updates.title: Confirmed-account trials
updates.notes: Your free 7-day trial now needs a confirmed canaanai.co.za account, which stops fake-email abuse. Sign up on the portal, click the confirmation link, then start your trial with that email.

updates.title: Trial licence keys
updates.notes: Every 7-day free trial now gets a real trial licence key, bound to your email and valid on one device only. Your key shows under Settings → Licence.

updates.title: Updated terms & conditions
updates.notes: Terms & conditions v1.1: the 90-day warranty clause is replaced by the free 7-day trial — evaluate LegalBill fully before you buy. The app shows the updated terms once for acceptance; everything else is unchanged.

updates.title: Version shown in the app
updates.notes: LegalBill now shows exactly which version you are running — on the sign-in screen and under Settings → Licence. Handy when checking you are up to date or asking for support.

updates.title: Secure online licensing
updates.notes: Licensing is now verified securely online. The 7-day free trial can no longer be reset, and licences activate with just your licence key and the email you bought with — no more device-code exchange. Each licence works on the number of devices its plan allows (Solo 2, Small Firm 5, Growing 12), and your firm can see and release activated devices in the Canaan AI portal. Your data is never touched. Buy or manage licences at canaanai.co.za/portal.html.

updates.title: 7-day free trial and device activation
updates.notes: LegalBill can now be tried free for 7 days on any new device - after that a friendly activation screen appears at sign-in and the app unlocks with your licence key plus a device activation code (your data is never touched while you activate). Licensed firms: your Settings > Licence card now shows this device's code and an Activate now link - send us the device code shown there and we reply with your activation code, which permanently unlocks that device. Buy licences or request activation at canaanai.co.za/portal.html or info@canaanai.co.za.

updates.title: Terms & conditions on installation
updates.notes: LegalBill now presents its licence terms up front: the installer shows a licence-acceptance page, and on first run each device is asked to read and accept a short terms summary (once-off perpetual licence, your data stays in your own database, support at support@canaanai.co.za). You can re-read the terms any time under Configuration settings > Licence > Terms & conditions.

updates.title: A tidier app - every section now folds away
updates.notes: Every major section across LegalBill is now collapsible and starts closed, so each screen opens as a clean summary and you expand only what you need. This covers the dashboard panels and charts, fee notes grouped by matter, trust transactions, the tax lists and income-tax cards, the compliance calendar, configuration settings, and the sections inside client, matter and fee-note detail views. Sections you open are remembered on your device.

updates.title: The premium icon now shows on your desktop shortcut
updates.notes: LegalBill's gold-framed icon - the one you already see in the top corner of the app window - is now embedded in the app itself, so your desktop shortcut, taskbar and Start menu show the LegalBill icon instead of a generic placeholder. The new icon appears automatically after this update installs.

updates.title: A warmer, more elegant sign-in - and a premium desktop icon
updates.notes: The sign-in page now carries the LegalBill brand look: a warm parchment panel framed in gold with the scales-of-justice motif, a classic navy serif headline and gold feature ticks, next to the sign-in card - replacing the previous deep-blue panel. The desktop icon has also been given a premium gold-framed finish, so LegalBill looks the part on your desktop and taskbar.

updates.title: Tax & Compliance centre - separate taxation for attorneys and advocates
updates.notes: The Tax tab is now a full South African tax compliance centre, and it is different in each office because attorneys and advocates are taxed differently. Set up your tax profile once (sole practitioner or incorporated at 27 percent, VAT registration and category, employees) and the app does the rest from the fee notes, payments and expenses you already capture. The new Income tax tab shows your taxable income live on either the accrual or cash basis, calculates income tax on the 2026 SARS tables, suggests your IRP6 provisional estimates from your actual figures, warns you before the 20 percent underestimation penalty, and records the payments you make. The Compliance calendar lays out every deadline for the year - IRP6 by 31 August and end February, the annual return by 31 January, VAT201 every two months with the net VAT already calculated from your records, and monthly EMP201 if you employ staff - each with a status chip you tick off as you file. Expense capturing now has searchable SARS-deductible categories tailored to your practice: chambers fees, Bar subscriptions and robes for advocates; staff, law library, indemnity insurance and more for firms. The app also watches the R1 million VAT registration threshold, reminds firms that trust money stays out of tax, flags bad debts, and warns advocates about the cash-flow trap of fee notes paid late in the year. Print the whole story as an Income tax pack PDF for your accountant.

updates.title: A brand-new sign-in experience and desktop icon
updates.notes: The sign-in page has been completely redesigned. A rich LegalBill brand panel now sits beside the sign-in card, telling the full story of your practice: matters and time-based fee notes, Bills of Costs with allocatur, trust accounting, VAT, and the separate Advocates Office with its own books - for attorneys and advocates alike. The sign-in card itself is cleaner and now uses LegalBill navy and gold throughout, with an encrypted / POPIA-aligned / cloud-synced trust strip, and remains proudly powered by Canaan AI. LegalBill also gets a brand-new Windows-style desktop icon - a navy letter tile beside a gold-accented fee note - so it looks right at home next to your other professional apps.

updates.title: True counsel billing in the Advocates Office; firm side simplified
updates.notes: The two practices now bill the way they really do in South Africa. On the Law Firm side, everything to do with advocates has been removed - counsel are not tracked as a directory or billed separately. When your advocate's fee note arrives, you simply capture it on the client's fee note as a DISBURSEMENT (the fee note form now reminds you of this), exactly as the referral system works: the attorney pays counsel and recovers it from the client. In the Advocates Office, billing no longer looks anything like attorney billing: there are no court tariffs at all. Every fee line uses a real counsel fee structure - Brief fee (preparation, consultation & heads of argument), Daily fee for court appearances (days x fee per day), Hourly work for consultations, research and drafting, Preparation fee, or a Fixed/collapsed fee - each with the standard wording filled in for you. The fee note prints as a proper counsel FEE NOTE addressed 'INSTRUCTED BY' your attorney, with the brief reference and matter, and terms of 7 days with a request to forward to the client for settlement. Advocates who sign in now go straight to their own office - the firm's books are never shown to them.

updates.title: Two separate practices - Advocates Office & Law Firm
updates.notes: Attorneys and advocates practise separately in South Africa, and LegalBill now works the same way. After signing in you choose where you are working: the Advocates Office or the Law Firm. The two offices are completely separate - each keeps its own clients, matters, fee notes, receipts, numbering, banking, logo and settings, and they never see each other's books. The Advocates Office is counsel's own chambers practice: instructing attorneys instead of clients, briefs instead of matters (numbered BRF-0001 onwards), fee notes numbered FN-0001 onwards at your own negotiated rates, and briefs can be recorded in every forum counsel appears in - the Constitutional Court, the Supreme Court of Appeal, the High Court and the Magistrates' Courts - with the correct court heading filled in for you. The Law Firm side keeps everything attorneys need: trust account, gazette tariffs, Bills of Costs and the counsel your firm briefs. Switch between offices any time from the account menu, and backups now say which office they belong to.

updates.title: Counsel billing - advocates sign in, bill the firm & track payments
updates.notes: Advocates can now sign in with their own login (Advocate role, linked to their advocate profile by email) and see a private billing workspace: capture fee notes to the firm using real counsel billing structures - hourly, day/brief fee, preparation, or fixed/collapsed fee, with optional VAT - and track exactly what has been billed, received and is still outstanding, plus the matters they are briefed on. They see only their own billing, never the firm's books. For attorneys and admins: the Advocates page now shows a firm-wide 'Owed to counsel' total, each advocate shows their unpaid balance, and every advocate's page lists their fee notes with billed/paid/owed figures - record payments to counsel right there. Every counsel fee note prints as a professional PDF from the advocate to the firm, numbered CFN-0001 onwards.

updates.title: Advocates section + role-based access for every user
updates.notes: New Advocates tab: keep a directory of the counsel your firm briefs - name, chambers, bar, specialisation, rate and whether they are a trust-account advocate. Link an advocate to a matter when you brief them; the matter shows who is briefed and the advocate's page lists all their briefs. Role-based access is now enforced on every action, not just tabs: each user's role (System Administrator, Attorney, Advocate, Bookkeeper, Secretary, Read-only) controls exactly what they can create, edit, delete and pay, and the Users & roles screen shows a full permission table for every role. New 'Advocate (Counsel)' role lets briefed counsel capture fee entries and reports without access to payments or the trust account.

updates.title: Calmer home page, auto-sync every few seconds & easier email setup
updates.notes: Home page: the charts are tucked away behind a 'Show charts' button and recent activity is now grouped under each matter, so you can see at a glance what happened on which case. My account: edit all your personal details (name, username, phone), not just your password. Court tariffs: the Government Gazette certificate wording printed on bills of costs and statements is now editable per court, so you can update it yourself when a new tariff notice is gazetted. Configuration settings: choose how often LegalBill auto-syncs with the cloud (every 5 seconds up to every 5 minutes, or off), and email/SMTP setup is much easier - type your email address and the server settings fill in automatically, with clear error messages and a test that saves your settings when it works.

updates.title: Home page numbers you can act on
updates.notes: The Home page summaries now tell you what matters right now instead of all-time totals. Received and Billed show this month's figures with an up/down trend against last month; Outstanding shows how much of it is overdue and on how many fee notes; Trust held shows how many clients the money belongs to. Click any summary card to jump straight to the page behind the number.

updates.title: Big usability update — search everything with Ctrl+K
updates.notes: LegalBill gets a major polish across every page. Press Ctrl+K (or click Search in the top bar) to instantly find any client, matter, fee note, estimate, receipt or trust transaction — or jump to any page — with full keyboard control. The Receipts and Estimates pages now have their own search boxes. Every popup uses the new styled dialog (no more plain browser popups, and this also removes a Windows quirk that could leave the app unresponsive after a popup). Dialogs open ready to type, Esc closes them, and the dashboard gains + New matter and + Trust transaction shortcuts.

updates.title: Sign-in screen now shows what LegalBill does
updates.notes: The sign-in screen now carries a short description of what LegalBill does — matters and billable time, fee notes, bills of costs, and trust accounting & VAT — set neatly beneath the tagline. The layout was tightened so the whole sign-in screen still fits without a scrollbar, even on smaller laptop displays.

updates.title: Smaller Canaan AI signature on the sign-in screen
updates.notes: The 'Powered by Canaan AI' signature on the sign-in screen is now smaller and sits as a modest footer, so it no longer competes with the 'Welcome back' heading. Matches the compact signature used in the LegalBill mobile app. No functional changes.

updates.title: Sign-in screen fits without scrollbars
updates.notes: The sign-in screen now fits fully on screen without scrollbars, even on smaller laptop displays — the layout was tightened and is vertically centred. The oversized tagline text baked inside the Canaan AI logo has been removed; the readable 'Intelligent solutions for Africa & beyond' line beneath the logo remains.

updates.title: Readable Canaan AI signature on the sign-in screen
updates.notes: Follow-up to v2.6.13: the 'Powered by Canaan AI' signature on the sign-in screen is now fully legible on the white login card. The 'Intelligent solutions for Africa & beyond' tagline is now shown as proper text beneath the Canaan AI logo (it was previously too small to read inside the logo), and the 'Powered by' label has been darkened so it stands out clearly. The support@canaanai.co.za contact is unchanged.

updates.title: Canaan AI branding on the sign-in screen
updates.notes: The sign-in screen now carries a professional 'Powered by Canaan AI' signature — the Canaan AI logo and the support@canaanai.co.za contact — beneath the sign-in form.

updates.title: Court dropdown on New Matter (and Bill of Costs)
updates.notes: When you register or edit a matter, the Court name field is now a searchable dropdown that matches the matter type: a High Court matter shows all South African High Courts, a Magistrate's Court matter shows the magistrate's courts, and a Miscellaneous matter shows no court field. The same searchable dropdown is also on the Bill of Costs screen so you can change the court there. Start typing to filter, pick one, or type your own. The list now appears reliably above the form instead of being cut off.

updates.title: Searchable court picker on the Bill of Costs
updates.notes: When generating a Bill of Costs, the Court heading is now a searchable dropdown. For a High Court matter it lists every High Court division and seat in South Africa; for a Magistrate's Court matter it lists the magistrate's courts nationwide. Start typing to filter (by town or division), pick one to fill the full heading, or just type your own court name if it isn't in the list.

updates.title: Fee notes grouped by matter + matter payments
updates.notes: Fee notes are now grouped under their matter everywhere, in collapsible sections you can expand to view or edit each one. High Court and Magistrate matters are billed and paid as a whole: the matter shows one combined total and balance, and you record a single payment against the matter (individual court fee notes no longer take their own payment). A court matter must be marked Finalised before you can record payment — matching the rule that court fees are paid once the matter is finalised in court; each matter payment produces a professional matter receipt. Miscellaneous matters keep their per-fee-note invoices and receipts. Dashboard, aging and 'received' totals now count court matters at matter level. The Bill of Costs taxing-master section has been redesigned as a proper Allocatur certificate.

updates.title: Bill of Costs — single continuous table
updates.notes: The Bill of Costs now shows the items and the taxation section as one continuous table: the grand total and the taxation breakdown (Less taxed off, Subtotal, VAT, Drawing fee, Disbursements, Attending taxation) follow directly under the last item, with the amount-in-words line and Taxing Master signature beneath. The firm's certificate — gazette citation, 'Dated at', signature and banking details — now appears last, at the very end of the bill. Applies to High Court and Magistrate bills.

updates.title: Bill of Costs taxation block + matter parties
updates.notes: Bill of Costs (High Court and Magistrate): the grand total no longer repeats at the bottom of every page — it now appears once at the end, followed by a taxation section (Less taxed off, Subtotal, Plus 15% VAT on fees, Add 11% drawing fee, Subtotal, Add disbursements, Subtotal, Add attending taxation) with an amount-in-words line and a Taxing Master signature. The blank amounts are completed by the court on taxation. When creating or editing a matter, applicants and respondents are now entered as separate name + role rows (1st Applicant, 2nd Applicant, and so on) with add and remove buttons, matching the Bill of Costs form.

updates.title: Login-after-logout freeze — real fix
updates.notes: Definitively fixes the login page being frozen after you sign out (you previously had to close and reopen the app). The sign-out confirmation used a native Windows pop-up, and after it closed the window could lose keyboard/mouse focus, leaving the sign-in page visible but unresponsive. Signing out now uses an in-app confirmation that stays inside the window, so the login screen always stays clickable and you can sign straight back in.

updates.title: Instant logout / login fix
updates.notes: Fixes the login page being frozen after you sign out (previously you had to restart the app). Signing out no longer waits on the network — the login screen appears immediately and is ready to use, and the session is cleared in the background.

updates.title: Update crash fix
updates.notes: Stops the app crashing with 'A JavaScript error occurred in the main process' when an update download does not complete. The installer launch is now fully guarded, incomplete downloads are retried instead of run, and a global safety net prevents any stray error from crashing the app.

updates.title: Update download fix (resume on drop)
updates.notes: Fixes the in-app update download freezing partway (e.g. stuck at 4%) on unstable connections. The download now automatically reconnects and continues from where it stopped instead of starting over, and retries several times. If it still can't finish, a 'Download in browser' link lets you fetch the installer manually.

updates.title: Login-after-logout fix
updates.notes: Fixes the login page being frozen/unclickable after you sign out (previously you had to restart the app). A leftover setup/overlay screen could sit invisibly on top of the sign-in form and swallow your clicks. The sign-in screen now always clears anything on top of it, so you can log straight back in.

updates.title: Freezing / performance fix
updates.notes: Fixes the app freezing. A background cloud-sync was accidentally re-triggering itself every 3 seconds, re-uploading all your data over and over and locking up the screen. Sync now runs only when something has actually changed, never while you are filling in a form, and the first sync after login is delayed so the app is usable immediately. Also caps the internal activity log so the app stays fast over time.

updates.title: Document polish
updates.notes: Three refinements to the generated documents: the 'TAX INVOICE / FEE NOTE' style heading at the top-right is now a smaller, brand-coloured size instead of oversized; the 'Amount' column heading is right-aligned so it sits exactly above its values; and the gold accent lines in the letterhead are thinner and more subtle. Applies to fee notes, receipts, quotes and statements.

updates.title: Premium document redesign
updates.notes: Fee notes, receipts, quotes and statements have been redesigned to look more professional. The firm name at the top no longer overlaps the document title, oversized text has been toned down, and the fee tables now have clean aligned columns with clear hairline rows. Totals are shown in a single elegant block (Subtotal, Total, Paid, Balance due). No data changes.

updates.title: Sign-in fix (critical)
updates.notes: Fixes sign-in, which was broken since v2.4.5: the application code inside the app was accidentally truncated during that release, so the sign-in button did nothing. The full code has been restored (and verified against the last known-good version). Includes the new clean white theme from v2.5.0. Please install this update.

updates.title: All-new clean white theme
updates.notes: A complete visual refresh inspired by the design of X: crisp white surfaces with hairline dividers, bold black headings, rounded pill buttons with a single blue accent, soft grey stat tiles, pill navigation in the sidebar, and a minimal white sign-in screen. Purely visual — no data or workflow changes. (Also repairs the update feed, which was malformed since v2.4.5.)

updates.title: Cleaner header & toned-down colours
updates.notes: UI refinements to the header and navigation: the crest icon has been removed for a cleaner look, the header is now a flat dark navy instead of a multi-stop gradient, the gold shimmer on the 'Bill' wordmark is replaced with a calm solid gold, primary action buttons throughout the app are now a consistent navy instead of the gold gradient, the active tab indicator is a flat navy underline instead of a glowing gold bar, and the account avatar is a quiet frosted circle. No data changes — install by double-clicking the installer.

updates.title: Design polish
updates.notes: Fixes the last rough edges of the new minimalist look: the dropdown arrow no longer tiles across the field (the row of chevron marks is gone), the header and login logo are now a terracotta scale mark that matches the theme instead of the old navy icon, and the Trust and VAT total cards are now clean white cards with the amount in terracotta instead of a heavy dark-brown block. Install by double-clicking the installer; your data is untouched.

updates.title: Fix startup crash + refined minimalist design
updates.notes: Fixes a 'JavaScript error occurred in the main process' crash on launch by loading the email module only when it is actually used, so a missing component can no longer stop the app from starting. Also a proper, cohesive pass on the new minimalist look: warm paper background, charcoal text and a single terracotta accent applied consistently to the header, sidebar, cards, stat tiles, buttons, chips, tables, segmented controls, inputs, modals and the login screen (the earlier version left several components with the old colours). Includes the restored sign-in code. Install this once by double-clicking the installer; your data is untouched.

updates.title: New minimalist design + sign-in fix
updates.notes: A clean, minimalist redesign: a warm paper background, charcoal text, a single muted terracotta accent, hairline borders and flat surfaces (no more heavy gradients, glows or glass blur) — across the whole app and the login screen. This release also restores the sign-in code that versions 2.3.0/2.3.1 accidentally dropped (they could not log in), and keeps the reorganised navigation (each Settings function in its own tab; Settings renamed Configuration settings) and the reliable update downloader. If you are stuck on the login screen, install this once by double-clicking the installer; your data is untouched.

updates.title: Reliable in-app update downloads
updates.notes: Fixes the in-app updater so downloads no longer freeze part-way (for example, stuck at 52%). A stalled download now times out cleanly, shows a clear message, and offers a one-click 'Download in your browser instead' option. Includes everything from 2.3.0: each Settings function has its own tab (Cost estimates, Receipts, Firm details, Billing settings, Court fee tariffs, User management, My account) and the Settings tab is renamed Configuration settings.

updates.title: Reorganised navigation — a tab for each area
updates.notes: The Settings area has been split up so each function has its own tab: Cost estimates, Receipts, Firm details, Billing settings, Court fee tariffs, User management and My account are now separate tabs in the sidebar. The old PDF-report shortcut was removed (client statements are still available from a client's page), and the remaining Settings tab is renamed Configuration settings. Firm details, Billing settings, Court fee tariffs, User management and Configuration settings stay administrator-only.

updates.title: Matters tab + reliable updates + full tariffs
updates.notes: New Matters tab to create and manage matters (High Court / Magistrate / Miscellaneous) with an automatic firm reference, a later court case number, court name(s) and applicant/respondent names that auto-fill the Bill of Costs; miscellaneous matters have no case number and everything is editable any time. Update detection now reads the real installed version and always fetches the latest manifest; existing installs receive the complete High Court (33) and Magistrate (148) gazette tariff lists via a one-time migration that keeps edited rates; and internal files were repaired for reliable startup.

updates.title: Full gazette tariff lists — every item
updates.notes: Completeness update to the fee tariffs. The High Court and Magistrate work-done dropdowns now contain every line item exactly as published in the 2025 gazettes, including the Sec 65/72/74 additional-costs disbursement and tracing lines, collection-commission percentages, the reserved slots (High Court D-04, Magistrate IV-25), Part I-05, and the counsel travelling-time line. High Court 33 items; Magistrate 148 items. Descriptions re-transcribed to match the gazette. All rates, caps and minimums editable under Settings.

updates.title: Complete court tariffs & Bill of Costs polish
updates.notes: The work-done dropdowns now contain every line item from the 2025 gazettes. High Court (Notice 52750): full sections A-F including Bill-of-Costs and taxation percentages. Magistrate (Notice 53149): full Table A Parts I-IV and Table B Parts I-III (Sec 65/72/74). Percentage items calculate from a base with min/max caps; reference-only items accept a manual amount; all items editable under Settings. Bill of Costs prints the gazette citation line, and the firm logo is larger on all documents.

updates.title: Court matters, tariffs & Bill of Costs
updates.notes: Major update for litigation billing. Create matters as High Court, Magistrate/Regional Court or Miscellaneous, each with an automatic firm reference and a court case-number field. Editable High Court and Magistrate fee-tariff tables under Settings. Fee notes take hours and minutes with a tariff work-done dropdown, plus per-line dates. Generate a full Bill of Costs per matter or a single invoice per fee note.

updates.title: Smarter first-time setup
updates.notes: Setup now asks whether this is your firm's first installation or a secondary device. First install runs the full flow; a secondary device connects to the existing firm database and goes straight to sign-in. Administrators create staff accounts with a temporary password that each user can change under My account.

updates.title: Modern UI/UX refresh
updates.notes: Complete visual refresh to modern standards with a refined design system, glass-blur header and tab bar, animated login, elevated cards, and smoother interactions.

updates.title: Print preview + real email sending
updates.notes: Print shows a full-screen PDF preview before printing. Email/Send delivers the document as a PDF attachment via your SMTP server, configured under Settings.

updates.title: New login page design
updates.notes: Redesigned login screen with animated glow, floating card, gold accent, logo header and password show/hide toggle.

updates.title: Print fix, Share sheet & Login freeze fix
updates.notes: Print works without pop-up permissions. Send/Share opens a full share sheet. Login no longer freezes on slow connections.

updates.title: Premium UI redesign
updates.notes: Redesigned interface with richer shadows, gradient header, gold-accented sidebar, polished cards, buttons and badges.

updates.title: Premium login redesign & bug fixes
updates.notes: New premium login screen, fixed inactive inputs after logout, update banner above sidebar.
